notes: Default whitespace parsing now matches the MMPDB fixture ID behavior.
Gzip SMILES input now matches the MMPDB fixture ID behavior.
Explicit tab parsing is covered in the Python API; MMPDB default CLI tab-file behavior remains accepted divergence.
OEMMPA exposes explicit delimiter modes in the Python API rather than reproducing every MMPDB default parser branch.
Comma parsing is explicit through delimiter="comma"; MMPDB default comma failure text remains CLI follow-up work.
OEMMPA supports configurable maximum-heavy-atom filtering in the fragmenter.
OEMMPA supports configurable maximum-rotatable-bond filtering in the fragmenter.
OEMMPA supports custom rotatable SMARTS for the rotatable-bond filter.
OEMMPA validates cut SMARTS shape for connected two-atom cut definitions.
OEMMPA already supports one through three cuts by default.
Cut R-group conversion remains deferred to a focused R-group follow-up.
Current C++ tests cover two-cut label behavior.

OEMMPA now stores unique OpenEye-native rule-environment rows without exact supporting-pair duplicates; current OpenEye fragmentation/orientation behavior produces 86 rules and 588 pair rows versus MMPDB's 47 and 342.
Phase 9 will add variable-size and symmetry filters.
Phase 9 will add index filter option parity.
OEMMPA can load properties with molecule records, but database refresh parity remains Phase 9.
OEMMPA refreshes rule-environment statistics after property loading and save; the 938 statistics rows are unique OpenEye-native single- and multi-attachment environment rows versus MMPDB's 533.
OEMMPA now matches input query molecules to compatible rule environments for supported non-hydrogen transforms; hydrogen transforms and CLI formatting remain separate follow-up work.
Property-specific rule-environment statistics and MP prediction are available through the Python API.
Phase 10 will add transform query output controls.
Query-constrained radius filtering now matches the pyridinol fixture for supported non-hydrogen transforms.
OEMMPA currently supports conservative variable-text substructure filtering rather than MMPDB's full SMARTS query behavior.
Query-constrained transform matching supports minimum pair count filtering.
Query-constrained transform matching supports safe count/radius where expressions; MMPDB's broader expression language remains intentionally unsupported.
Query-constrained transform matching supports explicit score modes and the MMPDB-style -min-radius alias.

CLI output design is deferred to a separate follow-up.
Rule-environment prediction matches the simple Cl to O MW and MP deltas through the Python API.
OEMMPA exposes selected rule-environment pairs through the Python API instead of writing MMPDB-style detail files.
MMPDB emits hydrogen deletion transforms that OEMMPA does not yet support.
OEMMPA summary recounts direct table rows and returns the same internal count categories needed for list recount behavior.
Phase 9 will add dataset option and count reporting internals before CLI list output design.
